source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure Oslogin Is Enabled for a Project

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Oslogin Is Enabled for a Project
input_row.description: Enabling OS login binds SSH certificates to IAM users and facilitates effective SSH certificate management.
input_row.rationale: Enabling osLogin ensures that SSH keys used to connect to instances are mapped with IAM users. Revoking access to IAM user will revoke all the SSH keys associated with that particular user. It facilitates centralized and automated SSH key pair management which is useful in handling cases like response to compromised SSH key pairs and/or revocation of external/third-party/Vendor users. Impact: Enabling OS Login on project disables metadata-based SSH key configurations on all instances from a project. Disabling OS Login restores SSH keys that you have configured in project or instance meta-data.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM compute metadata page by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/metadata. 2. Ensure that key enable-oslogin is present with value set to TRUE. 3. Because instances can override project settings, ensure that no instance has custom metadata with key enable-oslogin and value FALSE. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List the instances in your project and get details on each instance: gcloud compute instances list --format=json 2. Verify that the section commonInstanceMetadata has a key enable-oslogin set to value TRUE. Exception: VMs created by GKE should be excluded. These VMs have names that start with gke- and are labeled goog-gke-node
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the VM compute metadata page by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/metadata. 2. Click Edit. 3. Add a metadata entry where the key is enable-oslogin and the value is TRUE. 4. Click Save to apply the changes. 5. For every instance that overrides the project setting, go to the VM Instances page at https://console.cloud.google.com/compute/instances. 6. Click the name of the instance on which you want to remove the metadata value. 7. At the top of the instance details page, click Edit to edit the instance settings. 8. Under Custom metadata, remove any entry with key enable-oslogin and the value is FALSE 9. At the bottom of the instance details page, click Save to apply your changes to the instance. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure oslogin on the project: gcloud compute project-info add-metadata --metadata enable-oslogin=TRUE 2. Remove instance metadata that overrides the project setting. gcloud compute instances remove-metadata <INSTANCE_NAME> --keys=enable- oslogin Optionally, you can enable two factor authentication for OS login. For more information, see: https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/oslogin/setup-two-factor-authentication. Default Value: By default, parameter enable-oslogin is not set, which is equivalent to setting it to FALSE. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/managing-instance-access 2. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/managing-instance- access#enable_oslogin 3. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/compute/instances/remove- metadata 4. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/oslogin/setup-two-factor-authentication Additional Information: 1. In order to use osLogin, instance using Custom Images must have the latest version of the Linux Guest Environment installed. The following image families do not yet support OS Login: Project cos-cloud (Container-Optimized OS) image family cos-stable. All project coreos-cloud (CoreOS) image families Project suse-cloud (SLES) image family sles-11 All Windows Server and SQL Server image families 2. Project enable-oslogin can be over-ridden by setting enable-oslogin parameter to an instance metadata individually.
input_row.impact: Enabling OS Login on project disables metadata-based SSH key configurations on all instances from a project. Disabling OS Login restores SSH keys that you have configured in project or instance meta-data.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/managing-instance-access 2. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/managing-instance- access#enable_oslogin 3. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/compute/instances/remove- metadata 4. https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/oslogin/setup-two-factor-authentication Additional Information: 1. In order to use osLogin, instance using Custom Images must have the latest version of the Linux Guest Environment installed. The following image families do not yet support OS Login: Project cos-cloud (Container-Optimized OS) image family cos-stable. All project coreos-cloud (CoreOS) image families Project suse-cloud (SLES) image family sles-11 All Windows Server and SQL Server image families 2. Project enable-oslogin can be over-ridden by setting enable-oslogin parameter to an instance metadata individually.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control to ensure OS Login is enabled for a project can be audited using GCP APIs and CLI commands. The "audit" section specifies the use of the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) to verify the `enable-oslogin` key in the metadata. This clear direction towards an API-checkable property confirms that this audit can be performed programmatically without manual inspection.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_compute_project_os_login_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP API/CLI Commands:**
  - Use `gcloud compute project-info describe --format=json` to check project-level metadata for the key `enable-oslogin` with a value of `TRUE`.
  - Enumerate all VM instances in the project using `gcloud compute instances list --format=json`.
  - For each instance, verify metadata using `gcloud compute instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` to ensure the absence or correct setting of `enable-oslogin`.
  
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - The project metadata.
  - All individual GCP Compute Engine VM instances in the project.
  
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - At the project level: `commonInstanceMetadata` must include `"enable-oslogin": "TRUE"`.
  - At the instance level: Ensure no instance metadata has `enable-oslogin` set to `FALSE`, overriding the project setting.
  
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass**: The `enable-oslogin` is set to `TRUE` at the project level, and no instance has metadata overriding this setting.
  - **Fail**: The `enable-oslogin` is not set or is set to `FALSE` at the project level, or any instance has `enable-oslogin` explicitly set to `FALSE`.

This automated check ensures centralized and efficient evaluation of the compliance control, leveraging the capabilities provided by the Google Cloud SDK.
step2_review:
---
## 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** Correct. This control is well-suited for automation. Both project and instance metadata are accessible via GCP APIs and the `gcloud` CLI.
- **GCP APIs/SDK/CLI Feasibility:** The assessment correctly identifies that `gcloud compute project-info describe` and `gcloud compute instances describe` can retrieve the required metadata. No need for manual inspection.
- **Other GCP Tools:** GCP Config (now called Config Connector) does not natively monitor this metadata, but the CLI/API approach is standard and robust for this control.

---

## 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:**
  - **Project-level Check:** `gcloud compute project-info describe --format=json` is correct. The `commonInstanceMetadata` field contains the `enable-oslogin` key.
  - **Instance-level Check:** `gcloud compute instances list --format=json` to enumerate, then `gcloud compute instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` is correct for checking per-instance metadata.
- **Configuration Properties:**
  - Correctly identifies the need for `"enable-oslogin": "TRUE"` at the project level and that no instance should override this with `"FALSE"`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** Well-defined and aligns with CIS guidance.
- **Program Name:** Follows convention, but could be more descriptive (see Improvements).
- **Minor Omissions:**
  - Did not mention checking for `"enable-oslogin"` set to `"TRUE"` at the instance level (which is acceptable and should not cause a fail).
  - Did not specify handling of case sensitivity or whitespace in metadata values (should be robust in automation).
  - Did not mention the possibility of the key being absent at both project and instance levels (should fail if not present at project level).

---

## 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Program Name:**  
  - Current: `gcp_compute_project_os_login_enabled`  
  - **Suggested:** `gcp_compute_project_instance_oslogin_enforced`  
    - Rationale: Makes it clear that both project and instance-level settings are checked, and that enforcement (not just enablement) is the goal.
- **Additional Considerations:**
  - **Instance-level `"enable-oslogin": "TRUE"`:** Should clarify that this is acceptable and does not override the project setting negatively.
  - **Absence of Key:** Explicitly state that if the key is missing at the project level, this is a fail.
  - **Case Sensitivity:** Ensure automation is robust to case and whitespace in values.
  - **API Alternatives:** For large environments, consider using the [Cloud Asset Inventory API](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/overview) to enumerate instances more efficiently.
  - **IAM Permissions:** Note that the automation requires `compute.projects.get` and `compute.instances.get` permissions.
  - **Documentation Reference:** Reference the [official GCP documentation](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/oslogin/enable-oslogin) for clarity.

---

## 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound, aligns with GCP best practices, and uses correct API/CLI methods.

---

## 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Keep as Automated.**
- **Modify** to:
  - Clarify that `"enable-oslogin": "TRUE"` at the instance level is acceptable.
  - Explicitly state that absence of the key at the project level is a fail.
  - Suggest a more descriptive program name: `gcp_compute_project_instance_oslogin_enforced`.
  - Optionally, mention Cloud Asset Inventory API for scalability.

---

### **Summary Table**

| Section             | Verdict/Score | Notes                                                                                         |
|---------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE         | Correctly classified as Automated; feasible via GCP APIs/CLI                                 |
| Technical Accuracy  | 9/10          | Minor clarifications needed; otherwise technically correct                                    |
| Improvements        | -            | Clarify instance-level TRUE, absence of key, case handling, better program name, API options |
| Confidence          | HIGH          | Assessment is robust and aligns with GCP best practices                                      |
| Final Recommendation| Modify        | Minor clarifications and program name improvement                                            |

---
